steps.title: Recognize vertex form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = (x-2)^2 - 4$ is in vertex form. Here, $a = 1 \gt 0$ (opening upward), $h = 2$, and $k = -4$. An upward-opening parabola reaches its minimum at the vertex.

steps.title: Extract the minimum value
steps.content: The vertex is at $(h, k) = (2, -4)$, so the minimum value is $-4$. That is the correct answer.
